senses.definition: a type of unfriendly behavior that is meant to show that one is better than other people
a sense of high self-esteem or self-importance that one has

examples.x: Don't let success go to your head and cause a swelled head.
Ever since he won the award, he's had a swelled head and thinks he's better than everyone else.
Her constant bragging about her accomplishments shows her swelled head.

senses.definition: to behave in a way that makes others think one is more important or of higher status than one actually is

examples.x: Don't be fooled by his fancy clothes and sophisticated language; he's just putting on airs to seem more impressive.
He tries to impress people by putting on airs, pretending to be knowledgeable about topics he knows nothing about.

senses.definition: one's arrogant and pretentious behavior that is meant to prove one's superiority over others

examples.x: Don't get on your high horse with me.
I won't deal with you until you get off your high horse and stop patronizing me.
It's time you came down off your high horse and admitted you were wrong.
Larry is on his high horse again, bossing people around.
Players around Major League Baseball have mounted their high horses to belittle the Astros and commissioner Rob Manfred, who hasn’t punished those players in any way.
Trying to climb on any high horse would only be embarrassing at best, and at the very least hypocritical.

senses.definition: used to describe someone who takes too much pride or satisfaction in their achievements

examples.x: He dismisses everyone else's opinions, thinking he's always right, proud as Lucifer.
She flaunted her wealth and achievements, acting proud as Lucifer, looking down on others.
She strutted around the office, proud as Lucifer, after receiving the promotion.

senses.definition: an action that makes one feel more important than others

examples.x: During the meeting, he dominated the conversation, going on an ego trip and disregarding others' input
He flaunted his wealth and possessions, going on an ego trip to prove his superiority.
She always has to be the center of attention at parties, going on an ego trip to satisfy her need for validation.
She constantly talks about her accomplishments, always on an ego trip, seeking praise and admiration.
The celebrity's constant need for attention and validation is a clear sign of being on an ego trip.

senses.definition: used to describe someone who has an excessively high opinion of themselves

examples.x: He boasted about his family's wealth and connections, proud as a peacock, believing it made him superior to others.
He strutted around with his new car, proud as a peacock, eager to show off his latest possession.
She flaunted her achievements at every opportunity, as proud as a peacock, seeking validation from others.
She paraded around in her extravagant outfit, proud as a peacock, demanding attention and admiration.
The athlete stood on the podium, proud as a peacock, basking in the glory of winning the championship.

word_list.word: like the cat that (ate|swallowed|got) the canary
word_list.pronunciation: lˈaɪk ðə kˈæt ðæt ˈeɪt swˈɑːloʊd ɡɑːt ðə kənˈɛɹi

senses.definition: used for saying that a person looks extremely satisfied, happy, or proud because of something they have done or achieved
to fail to achieve success by having too much confidence in one's ability or position

examples.x: He overplayed his hand in the negotiation by demanding too much, causing the deal to fall through.
The company overplayed its hand by increasing prices drastically, resulting in a significant loss of customers.
The politician's attempt to gain more power backfired as she overplayed her hand and lost support from her constituents.

word_list.pronunciation: tˈuː bˈɪɡ fɔːɹ wˈʌnz bɹˈɪtʃᵻz ɔːɹ bɹˈiːtʃᵻz

senses.definition: behaving in a manner that highlights one's often non-existant superiority over others

examples.x: Ever since he won that small award, he's been acting like he's too big for his breeches, bragging about it to everyone.
He's been trying to take charge of every project, even though he's new to the team, acting like he's too big for his breeches.
She thinks she's the best singer in the world, but her performances show she's really just too big for her britches.
The young athlete's recent success has made him think he's too big for his breeches, ignoring the advice of his coaches.

senses.definition: someone who considers themselves more important than others and often behaves in a very formal and old-fashioned way

examples.x: Don't be a stuffed shirt, loosen up and enjoy the party!
John is such a stuffed shirt; he can't even crack a smile at a joke.
She dismissed the stuffed shirts in the office, preferring a more relaxed and collaborative work environment.
The boardroom was filled with stuffed shirts, all focused on maintaining a strict hierarchy.
The politician's speeches were filled with empty promises and stuffed shirt rhetoric.

senses.definition: to boost or satisfy one's sense of self-importance or self-esteem

word_list.pronunciation: hˈoʊld wˈʌnz hˈɛd ˌʌp hˈaɪ ɔːɹ

senses.definition: to take pride in one's deeds or achievements
